endpoints.name: Get API definition
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BBC_API_KEY}} with your BBC API KEY.

endpoints.name: Discover details of on-demand availability for programmes and their versions
endpoints.description: Discover details of on-demand availability for programmes and their versions
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BBC_API_KEY}} with your BBC API KEY.

endpoints.name: Build schedules and find metadata for TV and radio broadcasts
endpoints.description: Fetch metadata about linear Broadcasts and Services, allowing the generation of Television and Radio schedules and other datasets for broadcast items. Use /schedules instead of this feed as it is more efficient. Broadcasts will be deprecated in the future.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BBC_API_KEY}} with your BBC API KEY.

endpoints.name: Find metadata for curated groups: seasons, collections, galleries or franchises
endpoints.description: Long-lived curated collections of programmes and more, including Collections, Seasons, Franchises and Galleries
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BBC_API_KEY}} with your BBC API KEY.

endpoints.name: Find metadata for images
endpoints.description: Find metadata for images, particularly those in galleries
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BBC_API_KEY}} with your BBC API KEY.

endpoints.name: Look inside programmes to find segments: chapters, tracks and more
endpoints.description: Look inside programmes to find segments: chapters, tracks and more
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BBC_API_KEY}} with your BBC API KEY.

endpoints.name: List all Master Brands
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BBC_API_KEY}} with your BBC API KEY.

endpoints.name: Find the people behind and in programmes: cast, crew, guests and more
endpoints.description: The People feed allows you to search for the people and groups that contribute to programmes. This is the starting point for cast and crew credits, as well as finding contributors using external IDs (such as Wikipedia URLs)
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BBC_API_KEY}} with your BBC API KEY.

endpoints.name: Look inside pips entities
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BBC_API_KEY}} with your BBC API KEY.

endpoints.name: Exposes programme information for a single pid
endpoints.description: Exposes programme information for a single pid
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BBC_API_KEY}} with your BBC API KEY.

endpoints.name: Start here for programmes metadata: Brands, Series, Episodes and Clips
endpoints.description: Fetch metadata about Programmes (brands, series, episodes, clips). By applying different filter restrictions this feed can be used in many ways, for example to retrieve all series belonging to a brand, all the episodes and/or clips for a specific series, or any TLEO objects for a masterbrand. Other filters permit restricting to specific formats and/or genres, and you can request specific versions (for example Signed or Audio-Described). Parameters may be combined in any way suitable for your application.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BBC_API_KEY}} with your BBC API KEY.

endpoints.name: Discover metadata for content promotions
endpoints.description: Details of short-term editorially curated "promotions", for instance those programmes featured on iPlayer today
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BBC_API_KEY}} with your BBC API KEY.

endpoints.name: Build schedules and find metadata for TV and radio broadcasts and webcasts
endpoints.description: Dates, Times, Schedules: when and where are programmes being shown?
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BBC_API_KEY}} with your BBC API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get Schema definition
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BBC_API_KEY}} with your BBC API KEY.

endpoints.name: Information about the linear services used for broadcast transmissions
endpoints.description: The services feed exposes the linear broadcast "services" from PIPs. These are the actual services which broadcast programmes (eg bbc_one_oxford is the service for BBC One in Oxford).
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BBC_API_KEY}} with your BBC API KEY.

endpoints.notes: Replace {{BBC_API_KEY}} with your BBC API KEY. Replace {{PID}} with a real pid.

endpoints.name: Metadata on editorial programme versions: original, signed, audio-described, etc
endpoints.description: The versions feed exposes editorial "Versions" of programmes. These are concepts used to capture different presentations of an overall programme: for example, versions of a programme may include one with sign language, one with audio description, one edited for content and more. Versions are also important to understand for broadcasts: a linear broadcast or an ondemand is always of a specific version, not merely of a programme.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{BBC_API_KEY}} with your BBC API KEY.
